reasoning: Ariarne Titmus holds the 200m freestyle long course world record at 1:52.23, an exceptionally fast time set in June 2024. As of her recent retirement in October 2025, this record still currently stands. The 2025 World Aquatics Championships already concluded in July/August, meaning there are no global championship long-course events scheduled between the cutoff date (October 26, 2025) and the final resolution date (January 24, 2026). The international swimming calendar in late fall and early winter is largely dedicated to short course competitions or early-season, un-tapered long course meets (e.g., the U.S. Open in early December). Swimmers rarely break highly competitive long course individual world records mid-season, as that requires peak physical tapering typically reserved for national trials or summer global championships. The only active swimmer within striking distance is Mollie O'Callaghan (PB 1:52.48), but the lack of peak long-course events over the next three months makes a new record highly implausible. The small residual uncertainty primarily accounts for unforeseen anomalous mid-season performances or highly unlikely retroactive disqualifications.
